metadata_checks.comments: Title, DOI, year, and paper type match. Tax scope is appropriate for direct carbon/fuel-tax support, with subsidy reform used only as comparative context.

determinant_checks.comment: Matches the paper’s main result and directly concerns carbon-pricing support.
Paper reports a positive coefficient; extraction should encode '+' rather than n.a., even though p>0.10.
Paper reports a negative coefficient; extraction should encode '-' rather than n.a., even though p>0.10.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: tax cuts – income, labour, and consumption
determinant_checks.comment: Paper reports a positive coefficient; extraction should encode '+' rather than n.a., even though p>0.10.
Paper reports a negative coefficient; extraction should encode '-' rather than n.a., even though p>0.10.
Policy-type moderator is directly tax-related, but extraction leaves direction n.a.; reported coefficient is positive.
Context moderator is directly tax-related, but extraction leaves direction n.a.; reported coefficient is positive.
Regional fixed effect is directly reported and clearly positive in Table 1.
Regional fixed effect is directly reported and clearly positive in Table 1.
Regional fixed effect is directly reported and clearly positive in Table 1.

missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: Europe and West Asia (baseline: North America) 0.26 (0.69)
missing_tax_determinants.comment: Reported regional moderator is omitted from extraction; coefficient is positive but not significant.

final_recommendation_summary: Keep the paper in the corpus. The extraction is substantively on target, but several reported coefficients are left as n.a. despite clear positive/negative signs, and Europe and West Asia is missing.
